title: Director / Sr. Director, Creative & Production
description: About the Role This is the senior marketing leader who owns Seekr's brand and creative output as an integrated production function - brand visual identity, video production, motion design, photography, event creative, web visual standards, executive communications design, and the daily creative work that puts Seekr in front of buyers across every surface. The role lives at the intersection of brand strategy, creative direction, and production management. Video is the new center of gravity. Buyers increasingly evaluate AI providers through video - customer stories, product walkthroughs, executive thought leadership, demos, conference recordings - and Seekr's creative function needs to be built for that reality, not bolted onto an older content motion. You will own the video strategy, the video calendar, the production capability (in-house and agency-supported), and the standard for what ships. This is a manager-of-craft role with a real team. A Senior Manager, Brand & Creative reports to you, owning day-to-day brand stewardship and content production. A Senior Designer (hiring) reports to you, owning visual design across all surfaces. An external creative agency supports the function on video and other high-craft work; you direct that relationship as a buyer who knows what good looks like and can transition work in-house where the math favors it. The function reports to the CMO and partners closely with Technical Product Marketing on product visualization, Demand Generation on campaign creative, PR & Communications on earned content design and executive content, and Web & Digital on the web visual surface. Creative does not sit downstream of marketing; creative is one of the most credible expressions of Seekr's positioning, and you treat it as such. Duties and Responsibilities Own Seekr's brand visual identity end-to-end - the system, its application across every surface, and its evolution as the company scales. The brand is a tool, not a constraint; lead by using it well. Build and run the video production capability - strategy, calendar, in-house production, agency direction, post-production workflows. Treat video as a core competency, not a side project. Set a cinematic bar for Seekr's video quality - film-grade storytelling, pacing, sound, and color, not corporate-explainer templates. As an AI company, Seekr should be visibly working at the frontier of what AI makes possible in video; the work should make that obvious the moment someone hits play, without ever tipping into generic AI slop. Own the blend of AI-generated and live-action. The strongest work sits at the seam - generative sequences woven with real footage and b-roll, where AI extends what a camera could capture rather than replacing it. Know when a shot should be filmed, when it should be generated, and when the power is in the cut between the two. Direct the external creative agency relationship - scope, standards, budget, deliverables. Where the agency is delivering, scale the engagement. Where it isn't, manage to better outcomes or transition work in-house. Manage, coach, develop, and hire additional team members as scope warrants. Partner with Technical Product Marketing on product visualization - architecture diagrams, product walkthrough videos, technical illustration, demo design. AI architecture is hard to communicate visually; do it well. Partner with Demand Generation on campaign creative - concept, art direction, production. Where they own the campaign, you own the look, the feel, and the production quality. Partner with PR & Communications on executive content design, earned content visualization, and social creative - including the video and motion content that increasingly carries executive thought leadership. Partner with the GTM team on event creative - booth graphics, signage, presentation design, on-stage production for federal and commercial events. Partner with Web & Digital on the web visual standards and the web-as-brand-surface. Build and
score_reason: Senior creative leadership owning integrated brand, video, and production—perfect alignment with Travis's CD background and AI/production expertise, but no explicit remote confirmation or compensation details penalize slightly.

title: Head of Retention & Creator Partnerships
description: About The Nordstick The Nordstick makes innovative home fitness equipment, and we're growing triple digits year over year. We're a small, fast-moving team that wins on product and execution, not headcount or process. We've proven the product and the demand — now we're building the retention and creator engine that compounds it. This is a role for someone who wants real ownership and the room to build something from the ground up, reporting directly to the founder with no layers in between. The role in one line Own our owned channels (email + SMS), own affiliate and its profitability, and partner with our team to build a creator flywheel that turns product seeding and affiliate into a compounding, ROI-positive growth machine. We mean flywheel, not funnel. Outreach feeds creative, creative feeds performance data, performance data feeds smarter outreach — every loop should make the next one cheaper and better. If you build it right, it spins faster over time with less push. That's the job. What you'll own Email & SMS, end to end — strategy, calendar, flows, segmentation, list health, and the revenue these channels are accountable for. You set the roadmap and you're on the hook for the number. Affiliate profitability — not just recruiting affiliates and chasing volume, but owning contribution margin after commission and product cost. You'll know which partners actually make us money and which just look busy. The creator flywheel — sourcing and outreach, product seeding, briefing, white-label creative, performance review, and feeding what you learn back into who we work with next. You'll build the loop and keep it spinning with our team. Channel orchestration — making email, SMS, affiliate, and creator content work as one system instead of fighting over the same customer's attention and budget. What success looks like First 30 days: You've audited every flow, the affiliate program, and our current creator efforts, and you've come back with a prioritized list of where money is being left on the table. First 90 days: Owned channels have a clear roadmap and improving revenue, the affiliate program is being measured on real profitability, and the first version of the creator flywheel is live and producing. First year: The flywheel is compounding — creator outreach, seeding, and affiliate are demonstrably more efficient than when you started, and owned channels are a reliable, growing revenue line. You're probably right for this if You've been in the weeds — at an agency, a fast DTC brand, or both — actually building programs, not just managing dashboards or handing off to a team. You think in systems and loops, and you have strong, defensible opinions about what's worth doing and what's a waste of time and budget. You can tell a genuinely profitable creator from one with good-looking numbers, and you understand why most product seeding fails — and how to make it not. You've killed something that was working because something better existed, and you can explain why. You're comfortable owning a number and reporting directly to a founder who'll move fast with you. Tools — experience with a modern ESP/SMS platform (Klaviyo or similar), affiliate/creator platforms, and the analytics to actually measure profitability rather than vanity metrics. Logistics — Fully remote. Competitive base plus performance bonus tied to the growth you drive.
score_reason: Growth-stage DTC role with real ownership but lacks senior creative leadership scope, no remote/location clarity, and missing traditional CD/brand architecture—better suited for a Head of Growth than a Creative Director.

title: VP Product – Ads Experiences

At Fetch, we value curiosity, adaptability, and the confidence to explore new tools, especially AI, to drive smarter, faster work. This role will work closely with sister pillars for Growth, Ads Surfaces, Ads Platform, and Platform Foundations. Together, these leaders make Fetch the rewards destination for everyone, where users Live Rewarded, and partners reach their objectives. You’ll be joining Fetch at a pivotal moment as we expand rewarded commerce, introduce new earning formats, and scale our ads ecosystem across new verticals. Offer — If we're aligned, we'll move quickly. We aim to get from first conversation to offer in two weeks or less. We use structured feedback after every interview so decisions are based on evidence and consistency, not gut feel alone. title: Head of Performance Marketing, Agentic / AI-First

About The Role We're looking for a Head of Performance Marketing, Agentic / AI-First who operates like a builder, thinks like a scientist, and executes like a founder. Someone who writes their own landing pages, ships their own tests, and treats AI agents as the most important hire they'll ever make. This isn't a role for a paid media executive who manages managers. It's for an analytically rigorous, deeply curious operator who wants to rebuild how paid media runs at a modern B2B company — with Claude Code in one hand, a performance dashboard in the other, and a bias toward shipping. You'll take our strong B2B foundation and transform it into an AI-native growth engine: higher testing velocity, tighter feedback loops, better creative, and an operating model where a small team with great tools outperforms a large team with good ones. What You'll Own An AI-Native Paid Media Operation Own all paid channels — Search, Paid Social, Display/Programmatic, review sites, retargeting, and emerging platforms — and rebuild how they run from the inside. Use Claude Code, agents, and lightweight engineering to automate the work that today eats channel manager time: RSA generation with character-limit validation, SKAG structures, conversion upload pipelines, bid adjustments, audience syncs, creative QA, reporting. Stand up self-serve landing page infrastructure so campaigns don't wait on design or dev cycles. You should be comfortable shipping an LP yourself when the situation calls for it. Pioneer agentic workflows across targeting, messaging, platform ops, and optimization — not as a side project, but as the default way work gets done. Manage multi-million-dollar budgets with discipline while maintaining the velocity of a team several times your size. A Real Experimentation Engine Build the system that turns spend into learning. Design and run a high-throughput testing operation across creative, LPs, audiences, offers, and channels. Define clear hypothesis → test → read → scale-or-kill loops with honest statistical rigor. No vanity wins, no p-hacking, no narrative over data. Be the person on the team most willing to be wrong and fastest to update. Truth-seeking is the job. Use AI to 10x the rate at which variants get produced, shipped, read, and retired. The Operating Model Build the systems that let the team move fast without breaking things. Campaign templates, QA, taxonomy, routing — reimagined as code, agents, and lightweight tooling rather than wikis and checklists. Enablement for channel managers so they operate at the same velocity and quality bar you set for yourself. A shared standard for what "good" looks like across segments and regions. What You Bring 8–12+ years in Paid Media, Performance Marketing, Growth, or Acquisition — with a track record of building systems, not just running them. Hands-on fluency with AI tooling: Claude, Claude Code, ChatGPT, agents, and whatever ships next quarter. You've replaced meaningful parts of your own workflow with AI and can show your work. Comfort with light coding and technical systems: Python or JS, APIs, Google Sheets as a serious tool, scrappy scripts, JSON, landing page stacks. You don't need to be an engineer, but you can't be afraid of a terminal. Deep channel mastery paired with genuine creative sensibility. Y

title: Global Head of Marketing
description: Our mission We're making Africa the first cashless continent. In 2017, over half the population in Sub-Saharan Africa had no bank account. That's for good reason—the fees are too high, the closest branch can be miles away, and nobody takes cards. Without access to financial institutions, people are forced to keep their savings under the mattress. Small business owners rely on lenders who charge extortionate rates. Parents spend hours waiting in line to pay school fees in cash. We're solving this by building financial services that just work: no account fees, instantly available, and accepted everywhere. In places where electricity, water and roads don't always work, you can still send money with Wave. In 2017, we launched a mobile app in Senegal for cash deposit, withdrawal, and peer-to-peer and business payments. Now, we have millions of users across 9 countries and are growing fast. Our goal is to make Africa the first cashless continent and that's where you come in... How you'll help us achieve it We are hiring a Global Head of Marketing to drive growth across Wave’s Payments, Growth, and other core teams. You will report directly to Wave’s General Manager of Growth. Monitor metrics to measure marketing campaign effectiveness and optimize overall marketing performance over time. Continuously build and improve messaging across all channels and target audiences across various segments. Identify and close deals with marketing-specific partners. Optimize the cost of customer acquisition and own marketing budgeting: Collaborate across marketing to establish and manage marketing budgets for each country. Monitor spending to ensure resources are utilized effectively. Identify cost-saving opportunities and allocate budgets strategically to maximize return on investment. Optimize customer acquisition costs by working with our growth team to evaluate campaign performance and channel effectiveness. Manage Wave’s brand: Oversee brand efforts, ensuring consistent and impactful brand communications across all marketing materials and touchpoints. Key details This is a fully remote role. Candidates must be based in one of our talent hub countries (UK, Spain, USA, Canada, Kenya, and Ghana) or in one of our operating markets in Africa, including Senegal, Côte d'Ivoire, or Burkina Faso. Wave provides a yearly $1,200 stipend to support coworking meetups with teammates. Remote team members are expected to travel to our operational markets (e.g., Senegal or Côte d'Ivoire) at least once per quarter. Exceptions apply, but we’ve found this key to understanding our users and product. Our salaries are competitive and are calculated using a transparent formula. For this role, depending on your level and location, we offer a salary up to $195,000, plus a generous equity package Major benefits: Subsidised health insurance for you and your dependents, and retirement contributions (both vary from country to country). 6 months of fully paid parental leave and subsidised fertility assistance. Flexi

title: Head of Creative Growth
description: About Escargot Escargot started with greeting cards, but cards are the wedge. We’re building a new kind of digital-to-physical social network: one that helps people keep track of the relationships that matter, act on meaningful moments, and turn those moments into physical objects, gifts, cards, memories, and keepsakes. We believe people are craving more real, tangible, emotionally specific ways to connect. Social media became mostly performance and entertainment. Texting became the default for everything. Meanwhile, the things people actually save — cards, photos, notes, ticket stubs, fridge magnets, the weird little objects that prove a life happened — still carry disproportionate meaning. That is the space we’re building in: the bridge between digital convenience and physical meaning. We’ve raised $3M, we’re a small team, and people are already framing Escargot cards on their walls. Now we need someone to help make Escargot feel much bigger in the world. Currently operating across four partner firms with additional acquisitions underway, the platform is scaling toward a portfolio of 10–20 distinct brands and a $2B valuation target. The incoming Vice President of Marketing joins at a pivotal moment in the platform’s growth trajectory, with the opportunity to build the marketing function that will drive case acquisition performance across a rapidly expanding national portfolio. This executive will own case acquisition performance across the entire portfolio, develop and execute distinct marketing strategies for each brand, and build the marketing infrastructure required to support a rapidly growing, multi-brand platform. Core Profile: alignment with the following is required to get you in the conversation Multi-Brand Portfolio Marketing Leadership: The successful candidate will have actively managed marketing across multiple distinct brands, service lines, or market contexts simultaneously — not a single large firm with an unconstrained budget. Personal injury legal services is a trust-based, single-event professional service: the consumer is navigating a high-stakes decision after a stressful, unexpected event and will choose the firm they know and trust at that moment. Marketing in this environment is fundamentally different from marketing a product or a brand with repeat purchase cycles, and candidates must carry that understanding into every strategic decision they make. Each portfolio firm requires a custom strategy, a distinct messaging approach, and budget calibrated against approximately 20% of revenue as the marketing envelope per firm. The mandate is to maximize case acquisition performance within that constraint — not to flood channels with unlimited spend. Equally essential is the ability to think market by market and community by community: building 6 to 7 distinct approaches to capture market share across consumer demographics ranging from digitally sophisticated metro audiences to community-anchored, referral-driven markets where a baseball jersey sponsorship and a toy drive outperform any paid campaign. Digital-First Foundation Including Generative Engine Optimization: The platform’s marketing strategy has evolved beyond traditional SEO, and this is a non-negotiable technical threshold. Candidates must demonstrate a current, working understanding of Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) and AI-overview indexing — how content is structured for AI-driven discovery, not 2015-style long-form search optimization. Paid search experience (Google Ads, LSAs, PPC), organic search, social media advertising, and website optimization are required from day one. Beyond digital, the VP of Marketing oversees the platform’s full marketing spectrum: out-of-home advertising (billboards, transit, bus wraps), television and broadcast, radio, community outreach, sponsorships, and referral programs. Candidates do not need to be a specialist in every channel — but must be capable of evaluating performance, directing agency partners, and determining the right channel blend for each brand and market context. title: Director of Content Strategy / Brand & Content Lead
description: About the Company WorkMagic is a leading marketing measurement and experimentation platform helping e-commerce and retail brands make smarter, data-driven decisions across channels like Meta, TikTok, Google, and Amazon. Powered by AI and grounded in rigorous incrementality testing, we help brands uncover what truly drives results. We are growing rapidly, fueled by customer love, word-of-mouth, and bold ambitions. This is a team that solves problems from first principles, ships quickly, and obsesses over delivering measurable impact for our customers. The Problem Most marketing attribution is a lie. Platforms grade their own homework. Last-click models reward whatever touched the customer last, not what actually drove the sale. And billions of dollars in ad spend get allocated based on these distorted signals every single year. WorkMagic exists to fix this. We’re the incrementality measurement platform used by 100+ major D2C and ecommerce brands to understand what’s actually working — through geo-lift tests, multi-touch attribution, and cross-channel incrementality analysis. Our data reveals the truth that platforms don’t want marketers to see. The problem? Not enough people know this yet. The industry needs a wake-up call, and we need the person who can sound the alarm. The Role This is not a “write blog posts and manage the content calendar” job. This is a build the narrative and own the audience job. You will be the first dedicated content hire reporting directly to the CEO. You’ll define WorkMagic’s voice, build our thought leadership engine, and grow an owned audience that the industry pays attention to. You’ll have access to proprietary data from 100+ brands that no one else in the market has — your job is to turn that data into stories that change how people think about marketing measurement. What You’ll Own Narrative Strategy — Define the core story WorkMagic tells the market. Not a tagline. A worldview. Thought Leadership Content — Write original, opinionated pieces that make people stop scrolling. LinkedIn, long-form essays, industry commentary. Data Storytelling — Turn WorkMagic’s cross-client incrementality data into benchmark reports, industry indices, and data-driven narratives that earn press coverage and backlinks. Newsletter — Launch and grow a weekly/biweekly newsletter that becomes required reading for performance marketers and ecommerce leaders. Social Presence — Build WorkMagic’s LinkedIn and social channels into something people actually want to follow. Industry Influence — Get WorkMagic’s perspective into industry media (Marketing Brew, Digiday, Modern Retail, AdExchanger) through contributed pieces, expert commentary, and PR-worthy data releases. What This Is NOT SEO content production. We’re not trying to rank for “what is marketing attribution.” Generic B2B content marketing. No “5 Tips for Better ROAS” listicles. Demand gen support. You’re not writing landing page copy or nurture emails (though your content will absolutely generate demand). You You’ve built an audience before. Maybe it’s a newsletter with 2,000 engaged subscribers. Maybe it’s a LinkedIn presence that gets real engagement. Maybe you were the person behind a brand’s content that people actually talked about. The proof is in the work. You have strong opinions about marketing, measurement, or the ecommerce ecosystem — and you can back them up with reasoning and data. You’ve worked in or closely with the D2C/ecommerce/martech world. You know what a media buyer cares about. You understand ROAS, CAC, LTV, and incrementality — or you’re a fast, voracious learner. You write with a distinctive voice. Your work doesn’t sound like it was written by a committee (or a chatbot). You think in distribution, not just production. You know that a great piece of content nobody sees is just a diary entry.
score_reason: Senior narrative-building role reporting to CEO with creative scope, but SaaS/MarTech focus and missing remote confirmation + compensation details limit fit against Travis's advertising/brand DNA.

title: Creative Strategist
description: As this brand is scaling and spending 7 figures on Meta Ads monthly, creative performance is the single biggest growth lever for them. Our client is looking for a Creative Strategist who bridges the gap between raw performance data and the psychology of why people click, watch, and buy. You will lead a dedicated pod of creatives with a clear mission: systematically transform data into ads that actually scale. WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU True ownership : You have full autonomy to propose, prioritize, and approve the creative pipeline from start to finish. Clear career path : Both Senior Creative Strategist and Head of Creative seats are open; the brand prefers to promote high performers as they scale toward 9 figures. Compensation : Competitive salary, plus a performance bonus structure ranging from 30% to 50% of your base. Comprehensive benefits : Health, dental, and vision insurance, plus 2 weeks of PTO and major company holidays. Flexibility : Work 100% remotely with one flex day per quarter to recharge whenever you desire. WHAT YOU’LL OWN Develop and execute a cross-channel strategy (Meta, TikTok, AppLovin) that directly increases MER and allows for a 50% increase in ad spend. Fuel the growth engine by producing 20+ unique, high-quality concepts every week through the delivery of 4 videos and 3 static briefs daily. Lead a team of 4 Video Editors and 1 Graphic Designer to achieve same-day feedback loops, ensuring zero friction in the transition from brief to live asset. Convert complex data from Ads Manager and Motion into actionable creative iterations, moving from "opinion-based" creative to "winning-pattern" execution. Produce high-converting ad scripts and short-form copy that leverage VSL and UGC formats to lower CPAs and stabilize top-of-funnel performance. Build and refine creative testing frameworks that eliminate waste by identifying "winners" early and scaling them systematically across the account. WHO YOU ARE You have a verifiable track record in the Facebook Ads Library, ideally with 20+ active ads for marketing-first consumer brands. You are comfortable navigating Ads Manager to analyze Hook CTR, Hold Rate, and CPA, but you can also write a compelling 5-minute ad script. You know how to get the best out of editors and designers, giving clear, direct feedback that raises the creative bar. You have deep experience in problem-solution messaging and direct-response copywriting - you can diagnose why a script failed, not just that it did. You are a self-driver who thrives in a high-accountability environment and prefers results over conceptual "brand" fluff.

Today, our colorful and thoughtfully designed products including the popular Quencher™ series go beyond function, elevating everyday moments with style and empowering active lifestyles. We’re a community of creators, builders, and inventors who believe in creating sustainable products for a better life and world that minimizes our impact on the planet. Learn more at www.stanley1913.com. Position Overview Design and Innovation are the heartbeat of our brand. We are seeking a Global Creative Design Director ; a culturally attuned leader across beauty, fashion, sport, and food and beverage. A leader who will drive the future of our color, concept, and print vision while celebrating the heritage of the Stanley 1913 brand. A design catalyst and a future thinker, this role is for a product leader who blends consumer insights, creative direction, color and concept leadership and cultural relevance to shape the overall seasonal Design Direction for Stanley 1913 products. From concept through craft, ensuring creative direction is modern, culturally relevant, and unmistakably iconic. They will serve as a connector between Design and creative partners across the Stanley 1913 ecosystem, leading with consumer compassion, clarity of vision, and creative courage, reporting to the VP of Innovation and Design. ***Must be located in WA, OR, or CA. Please include portfolio in application.*** What you’ll do Define and evolve Stanley 1913’s visual design language, ensuring consistency and excellence across product touchpoints. Own the creative vision for Softgoods and Hardgoods, partnering with Insights, Innovation, ID, CMF, and Design Leadership to identify trends and shape seasonal direction. Drive Global Product Creative Direction from concept through launch, ensuring alignment while maintaining a distinct design identity. Develop a cohesive seasonal color strategy across core, seasonal, and novelty collections. Collaborate with Express Lane and Partnerships teams to drive brand relevance and speed-to-market execution. Partner cross-functionally with Design Leadership, Merchandising, Product Management, and Brand Creative to ensure alignment and impactful storytelling. Present creative work to senior leadership, clearly articulating vision, rationale, and business impact. Champion a culture of curiosity, experimentation, and continuous design improvement. Balance critical thinking with hands-on creative leadership and execution. Stay current on trends in fashion, design, technology, culture, and sport through the lens of the Stanley 1913 consumer. Lead, mentor, and develop the color and concept design team. Who you are Strong understanding of the Stanley 1913 brand and competitive landscape. 10+ years of demonstrated ability leading concept, design, or product design direction. 5+ years as a people leader Proven ability to translate fashion, sport, and culture into compelling global creative direction. Experience developing brand-defining creative direction informed by trends, insights, and cultural influence across style and food culture. Ability to translate insights into seasonal design direction in partnership with Innovation, CMF, and Industrial Design teams. Hands-on creative leader with exceptional taste and a clear point of view, able to operate from concept through execution. Strong presentation and influencing skills, with confidence engaging executive leadership. Ability to analyze and apply trends in color, materials, and innovation. Excellent communicator with a track record of effective cross-functional collaboration. Experienced mentor with a passion for developing talent and building impactful teams. Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite; AI experience is a plus. Bachelor's degree in design, BFA, or a related field, or equivalent
score_reason: Senior creative leadership title with global scope, but product/design-focused (not advertising/brand strategy) and no explicit remote policy stated—appears office-based given product design nature.

title: VP of Marketing - Head of Marketing
description: About Us Hyprwork is a fast-growing operator of direct-to-consumer brands in health and wellness. Our flagship brand, Rejuvacare, serves over 400,000 customers who trust us with their health and their money. We ship primarily to the United States, offer a 90-day money-back guarantee, and run a RejuvaCare+ membership program that powers recurring revenue. The company has scaled 12x in a single year to nearly 150 people across 15+ countries. We are remote-first, async-first, and hold a high standard of accountability across everything we do. The Role This is the seat that runs marketing for Hyprwork as the company transitions from a founder-led operation into a multi-brand holding. You replace the operational footprint of our outgoing CMO, who moves into a founder and visionary role alongside our CEO. You own marketing strategy, execution, and P&L for the entire Hyprwork portfolio, today centered on Rejuvacare and expanding into new brands over the next four to eight quarters. This is not a strategy-only seat or a corporate CMO post. Hyprwork is a performance-first DTC business that has scaled 12x in a single year on paid acquisition discipline, brand execution, and operational excellence. The CMO here is expected to know the numbers, run the team hands-on, and build the structure that lets marketing scale beyond the founders' direct involvement. You inherit a marketing organization with an established performance marketing function (paid acquisition under five channel pod leads), a creative production engine producing hundreds of assets per week, and developing brand and lifecycle capabilities. You own the design of the next layer of marketing leadership and the build-out as the brand portfolio expands. The founder transition is the defining context of this role. Our founders are stepping back from day-to-day marketing operations but remain deeply invested in brand direction, portfolio strategy, and company outcomes. This is not a clean handover into a vacuum. You are stepping into a seat previously operated by a founder who is becoming your closest strategic partner. The first three to six months are a trust-building phase. You will collaborate closely, demonstrate judgment, and earn the decision rights of the seat before operating fully independently. Comfort with that dynamic, the patience to navigate it, and the maturity not to mistake collaboration for interference are non-negotiable. What You Will Own Marketing Strategy and P&L You own marketing strategy across the Hyprwork portfolio: brand direction, channel mix, budget allocation, customer acquisition and retention strategy, and the P&L for marketing spend. You set the annual and quarterly marketing plans, defend them in the executive leadership team, and deliver against them. Performance Marketing and Growth Paid acquisition is the engine of this business. You own the performance marketing function strategically, with five channel pod leads executing tactically across Meta, Google, Native, AppLovin, and TikTok. You set acquisition targets, CAC and LTV thresholds, channel diversification strategy, and the operational standards that make paid acquisition sustainable as the business scales. You read the dashboards, know the numbers, and challenge them. Creative Function You own the creative production machine that feeds every paid channel. A Creative Manager leads the function with sub-leads across video editing, AI video, graphic design, and copy. The team produces 400+ videos and 250+ images per week to feed the testing system. You hold the Creative Manager accountable for throughput, win rate, and quality. You set the creative strategy that gives the pod leads the at-bats they need to scale revenue. Brand, Lifecycle, and Customer Marketing You own how Hyprwork's brands show up across every customer touchpoint and how they compound value beyond the first purchase. This includes brand identity and standards, lifecycle and retention programs, RejuvaCare+ membership market
score_reason: Senior CMO-equivalent role at remote-first company with strong creative + performance scope, but DTC/performance-marketing focus and missing salary/currency details create minor friction against Travis's brand/integrated campaign background.

The goal isn't brand-as-decoration. It's brand as compounding advantage: the reason a great engineer joins us over a competitor, the reason a CIO remembers our name in a procurement meeting, the reason a journalist picks up our story. This is a senior creative and strategic role. The best work will come from real partnership across all three seats. Who You Are You have strong opinions, calmly held, about what's good. You can tell the difference between work that's correct and work that's alive , and you can articulate why. You read widely (design, fashion, music, film, architecture, fiction, technology) not because it's your job to, but because it's how you think. You don't need to come from tech. We'd actually prefer you didn't, fully. The strongest candidates for this role have spent time inside great creative environments: culture-forward consumer brands, world-class agencies, magazines, design studios, record labels, fashion houses, museums. You want to bring that sensibility into a fast-moving B2B AI company. What's non-negotiable is that you can operate inside that environment without losing your taste, and that you can translate a complex AI product into something a human being feels something about. About The Role Cohere is building frontier AI for the enterprise. The product is technical. The buyer is technical. The category is loud. None of that exempts us from being a brand people actually want to be near. You'll be the senior leader most responsible for what Cohere feels like in a keynote, on a billboard, in a product launch film, on a t-shirt at a conference, in a post that goes around design Twitter on a Sunday night. The goal isn't brand-as-decoration. It's brand as compounding advantage: the reason a great engineer joins us over a competitor, the reason a CIO remembers our name in a procurement meeting, the reason a journalist picks up our story. This is a senior creative and strategic role. You'll set the brand strategy, own our narrative and verbal identity framework, drive our cultural point of view, and partner closely with two creative counterparts who report into the same leader: our Design Director, who leads in-house design and owns Cohere's visual identity, and our Editorial Director, who leads our owned-media, long-form content, and the execution of voice across what we publish. Together, the three of you are the taste filter for everything that goes out the door under the Cohere name. Your job is to set the vision and the brief; theirs is to bring it to life. The best work will come from real partnership across all three seats. Who You Are You have strong opinions, calmly held, about what's good. You can tell the difference between work that's correct and work that's alive , and you can articulate why. You read widely (design, fashion, music, film, architecture, fiction, technology) not because it's your job to, but because it's how you think. You don't need to come from tech. We'd actually prefer you didn't, fully. The strongest candidates for this role have spent time inside great creative environments: culture-forward consumer brands, world-class agencies, magazines, design studios, record labels, fashion houses, museums. You want to bring that sensibility into a fast-moving B2B AI company. What's non-negotiable is that you can operate inside that environment without losing your taste, and that you can translate a complex AI product into something a human being feels something about. What You'll Own Brand strategy and narrative Set the brand strategy: who Cohere is, what we stand for, what we won't be. Verbal identity and creative partnership Own Cohere's verbal identity at the framework level: the voice and tone strategy, copy systems, naming conventions, the brand book. The Editorial Director owns the execution of voice in long-form published work, drawing from the framework you set. Part

Your job is to take what's in motion, raise the standard, push the ambition, and give the function a sharper editorial center of gravity. This is not a content marketing role. We don't need someone to scale a case-study library or run a blog calendar tuned to SEO. We need someone who can build a real editorial property. The kind of work people read on a Saturday morning because they want to, not because they were retargeted into it. Think Stripe Press. The early years of Airbnb's magazine. Mailchimp's Courier. MIT Technology Review at its best. A24's editorial output. That register. You'll own Cohere's editorial vision, our publishing calendar, our writers (staff and freelance), and our long-form output across writing, film, photography, podcasts, and eventually books. You'll be a peer to our Head of Brand Marketing and our Design Director. The three of you, reporting to the VP of Brand, set the creative direction of the company. You're our head of storytelling, externally and internally. Externally, you build the editorial property and the body of work we publish under our name. Internally, you help the company tell its own story to itself, so the people making the work can feel what they're a part of and why it matters. The role sits between art and science. The art is editorial taste: knowing what story is worth telling, who should tell it, and what register it lives in. The science is publishing discipline: running a real calendar, shipping on cadence, commissioning and editing at volume without losing the standard, and knowing how a piece actually finds its audience. We need both. People who can do one but not the other will struggle here. Who You Are You're an editor in the old sense of the word. And the new sense too. The old-school part: you can read a draft and tell the writer what it's actually about, including the parts they hadn't realized yet. You commission well. You can talk about a piece of writing at the sentence level and at the strategic level in the same conversation. The new-school part: you think in formats, not just words. A story might want to be a 4,000-word essay, a short film, a photo series, a newsletter, a podcast, a thread. You know which form a story belongs in, and you know how a single idea can travel across surfaces without losing itself in translation. You don't own social (the Social Lead does), but you watch it closely, you understand how it shapes contemporary publishing, and you know that what shows up there is often the public face of the editorial slate. Above all, you're an integrator. You can look at a blog, a newsletter, a film series, a podcast, and a feed and see whether th

title: Senior Director, Membership

From students and practitioners to executives and academics, we aim to elevate the profession, deepen knowledge, and make a lasting impact. The AMA is home to five premier scholarly journals, including: Journal of Marketing , Journal of Marketing Research , Journal of Public Policy and Marketing , Journal of International Marketing , and Journal of Interactive Marketing . Our industry-leading training events and conferences define future-forward practices, while our professional development and PCM® professional certification advance knowledge. With 70 chapters and a presence on 350 college campuses across North America, the AMA fosters a vibrant community of marketers. The association's philanthropic arm, the AMA’s Foundation , is inspiring a more diverse industry and ensuring marketing research impacts public good. AMA views marketing as the activity, set of institutions, and processes for creating, communicating, delivering, and exchanging offerings that have value for customers, clients, partners, and society at large. You can learn more about AMA’s learning programs and certifications, conferences and events, and scholarly journals at AMA.org . However , please note that you MUST live in one of the following states to be considered for this role: Alabama, Colorado, District of Columbia, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Indiana, Louisiana, Massachusetts, Michigan, Minnesota, North Carolina, New Jersey, New York, Ohio, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, Washington, or Wisconsin! We are unable to negotiate higher than this range. Please note that only applicants who apply officially will be considered in this process. We appreciate the candidates who apply and express interest, but we cannot circumvent the recruitment process for any candidates, regardless of their relationship to the AMA or our team members. title: Senior Director, Head of Trademarks
description: This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Director, Head of Trademarks in United States. This executive-level role offers the opportunity to lead and shape a global trademark strategy for a highly recognized international brand portfolio. You will oversee complex trademark operations spanning pharmaceutical, consumer, and medical product categories across multiple jurisdictions worldwide. The position combines strategic legal leadership with operational innovation, leveraging modern technologies and AI-powered tools to enhance portfolio management and brand protection efforts. Working closely with cross-functional teams across legal, regulatory, commercial, and R&D functions, you will play a central role in safeguarding intellectual property and supporting long-term business growth. The role also involves leading anti-counterfeit initiatives, advising senior leadership, and driving trademark strategy in a fast-moving global environment. This is an exceptional opportunity for a seasoned trademark attorney who thrives at the intersection of law, innovation, and global brand strategy. Accountabilities Lead and manage a large-scale global trademark portfolio, including pharmaceutical product names, consumer brands, trade dress, and medical or surgical product marks across numerous international jurisdictions. Develop and execute comprehensive trademark strategies covering clearance, prosecution, maintenance, enforcement, and portfolio optimization aligned with commercial priorities. Oversee trademark clearance and naming strategies for new pharmaceutical and consumer products in collaboration with regulatory, marketing, medical, and commercial stakeholders. Implement and enhance AI-driven trademark management processes and automation tools to improve operational efficiency and portfolio oversight. Direct global relationships with external trademark counsel, ensuring strategic consistency, quality, and cost management. Design and lead anti-counterfeit and brand protection initiatives across online marketplaces, social platforms, customs enforcement, and litigation channels when necessary. Serve as the primary advisor on trademark-related matters to senior leadership, marketing teams, R&D, business development, and regulatory functions. Support mergers, acquisitions, and post-acquisition intellectual property integration activities. Build, mentor, and lead a high-performing trademarks team while fostering a proactive and collaborative legal culture. Present portfolio performance, enforcement strategies, and emerging risks to executive leadership and board-level stakeholders. Requirements Juris Doctor (J.D.) degree from an accredited law school with active U.S. bar membership required. Minimum of 7 years of trademark law experience, including substantial experience in-house or advising global pharmaceutical, consumer goods, or medical device organizations. Deep expertise in pharmaceutical and consumer product naming, trademark clearance, and global trademark portfolio management. Demonstrated experience managing large and complex international trademark portfolios, ideally exceeding 10,000 marks. Strong understanding of anti-counterfeit enforcement strategies, including online platform enforcement, customs recordation, and related legal actions. Experience leveraging AI-powered trademark tools, automation technologies, or advanced trademark management platforms such as Anaqua. Proven ability to collaborate effectively across legal, commercial, regulatory, marketing, and executive teams. Strong leadership, mentoring, and team management capabilities. Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to advise executive stakeholders on strategic IP matters. Experience supporting mergers, acquisitions, and post-merger IP integration activities in multinational environments is highly valued. Benefits Competitive salary range of approximately $280,000-$320,000

title: Senior Director, Head of Trademarks
description: Bausch + Lomb (NYSE/TSX: BLCO) is a leading global eye health company dedicated to protecting and enhancing the gift of sight for millions of people around the world—from the moment of birth through every phase of life. Our mission is simple, yet powerful: helping you see better, to live better. Our comprehensive portfolio of over 400 products is fully integrated and built to serve our customers across the full spectrum of their eye health needs throughout their lives. Our iconic brand is built on the deep trust and loyalty of our customers established over our 170-year history. We have a significant global research, development, manufacturing and commercial footprint of approximately 13,000 employees and a presence in approximately 100 countries, extending our reach to billions of potential customers across the globe. We have long been associated with many of the most significant advances in eye health, and we believe we are well positioned to continue leading the advancement of eye health in the future. We are seeking a highly accomplished and strategic trademark attorney to serve as Senior Director, Head of Trademarks . This is a rare opportunity to own and shape the global trademark function for one of the world's most iconic eye health brands. You will manage a large, complex portfolio spanning pharmaceutical, consumer, and surgical product categories, partnering closely with brand, regulatory, commercial, and legal teams across the enterprise. This role reports to senior legal leadership and carries significant influence over brand protection strategy globally. Develop and implement portfolio strategy including clearance, prosecution, maintenance, and enforcement priorities aligned to commercial objectives. Direct enforcement actions including cease-and-desist campaigns, platform takedowns, Customs recordation, and, where appropriate, civil litigation or criminal referrals. Collaborate with supply chain, regulatory, and government affairs teams on brand protection and product authentication initiatives. Stay current on emerging counterfeit threats in the ophthalmic pharmaceutical and consumer lens care space and adapt enforcement strategy accordingly. Cross-functional leadership Act as the primary legal advisor on trademark matters to brand, marketing, commercial, regulatory, and R&D teams globally. Partner with M&A and business development teams on IP due diligence and post-acquisition trademark integration. Build and mentor a high-performing internal trademarks team and foster a culture of proactive brand protection. Present regularly to senior leadership and the board on portfolio health, enforcement activity, and emerging risks. Qualifications J.D. from an accredited law school and active bar membership (U.S. required; additional jurisdictions a plus). 7+ years of trademark law experience, with a meaningful portion in-ho

the function Ensure the department has a well-organized infrastructure and adequate resources to enable projects to be completed on time and within budget Independently lead and represent Viridian Biometric Sciences for all statistical programming of an assigned study or assigned studies across programs Accountable for compliance with regulatory and ICH guidelines, GCPs, company guidelines and Standard Operating Procedures Establish rapport and maintain positive, open communication with internal Viridian and external third-party teams Effectively manage internal statistical programmers, statistical programming deliverables ensuring audit best practices have been ensure the quality and assurance of statistical content and supporting documents are validated Support user acceptance presentation plans Management of all ongoing cross-functional data reviews throughout the conduct of the study to ensure timely and appropriate identification of errors and discrepancies Accountable programming function to senior management Participate in Biometric sciences initiatives such as evaluate technology vendors (i.e. analytic tools), author and review and revise SOPs, and establish data title: Director of Outbound Growth (USA - Remote)

Our global USD account infrastructure and payment APIs are used by fintechs, embedded finance platforms, and financial institutions moving money across LatAm, Africa, and beyond. We believe in a world of fair economic opportunity, where global commerce can flourish through the frictionless exchange of value. Our first steps toward that vision are enabling drastically faster, cheaper, and more reliable transactions for businesses in emerging markets — and we're backed by investors who share that conviction, including Dragonfly, Circle, and Coinbase. We're here to build, and we mean that. We empower our team to act like founders, make bold decisions, and deliver outcomes that matter. With our brand-new global USD product and Series A round behind us, we're ready to build a world-class revenue engine. We believe the future of outbound is agentic and AI-native, and we're building accordingly. As Director of Outbound Growth, you will design, build, and permanently own Conduit's AI-native outbound engine — a system that operates at scale through intelligence, not headcount. You'll own the full stack from ICP definition and signal identification to enrichment, personalized sequencing, and pipeline reporting. Collaborate with the CBO on messaging strategy, positioning, and launch campaigns tied to the USD API. Pipeline Ownership. Deep fluency in the modern outbound stack: Clay, Smartlead or Instantly, Apollo or ZoomInfo, HubSpot or Salesforce, LinkedIn Sales Navigator AI-First Mindset (Hard Requirement). Strong AI-first orientation — you use AI to write, enrich, personalize, and optimize at every layer of the outbound motion Fintech Fluency. Background in fintech, payments, or financial infrastructure strongly preferred — you understand the buyer, the language, and the competitive landscape Writing Quality. Exceptional written communication — you write outbound copy that gets replies, not spam filters. Systems Thinking. Analytical and systems-oriented — you think in workflows, test hypotheses, and optimize based on data IC Orientation. description: We are representing a high-growth healthcare AI company that is transforming revenue cycle operations for enterprise provider organizations. The company is looking for a Chief Customer Officer to lead its full post-sale customer organization and build the operating model required to scale enterprise implementations, accelerate ARR realization, strengthen customer trust, and turn customer delivery into a strategic growth engine. This is a CEO-facing executive role with significant influence across Product, Engineering, Commercial, Compliance, and Operations. Key Responsibilities Executive Leadership Lead and scale the full customer organization across implementation, customer success, support, coding operations, compliance operations, and related customer-facing functions. Build a unified post-sale operating model with clear ownership, governance, metrics, accountability, and executive visibility. Serve as a strategic member of the executive leadership team, helping shape company priorities, growth strategy, operational execution, and customer outcomes. Develop the organizational structure, leadership bench, talent strategy, and operating rhythms required to support rapid enterprise growth. Create a culture of urgency, accountability, customer obsession, operational excellence, and cross-functional collaboration. Required Experience 15+ years of leadership experience in healthcare technology, SaaS, revenue cycle management, healthcare AI, healthcare services, or another complex enterprise healthcare environment. Proven experience leading and scaling large customer-facing organizations, including implementation, customer success, support, delivery, operations, or professional services. Experience operating in high-growth environments where customer delivery, revenue realization, and organizational scale are critical. Strong understanding of healthcare provider workflows, revenue cycle operations, enterprise implementation complexity, and customer trust requirements. Demonstrated success building scalable post-sale operating models, customer governance frameworks, delivery metrics, and executive reporting. Experience managing complex executive customer relationships with health systems, hospitals, provider organizations, or other enterprise healthcare customers. Strong ability to partner with Product and Engineering teams in technical, fast-moving environments. Proven ability to lead cross-functional transformation and influence teams outside of direct reporting lines. Excellent executive communication, operating cadence, and decision-making skills. Customer Journey and Operating Model Transformation Redesign the end-to-end customer lifecycle from pre-implementation handoff through deployment, adoption, value realization, renewal readiness, and expansion support. Create repeatable implementation methodologies, deployment playbooks, customer governance models, and operational standards that improve predictability and scale. Reduce implementation variability, unnecessary customization, customer escalations, and operational friction. Build a customer operating system that provides clear visibility into deployment health, adoption progress, risks, milestones, executive engagement, and value realization. Ensure customers experience a consistent, high-quality journey while preserving the flexibility required to meet the needs of complex enterprise healthcare environments. Healthcare Operations, Coding Quality, and Compliance Excellence Ensure the customer organization operates with the quality, rigor, transparency, and compliance discipline required in enterprise healthcare environments. Partner with Product, Clinical, Compliance, and Operational leaders to improve implementation readiness, specialty deployment quality, customer confidence, and operational scalability. title: VP, Growth, Strategy, and Donor Relations

Long Summary PolicyLink advances structural solutions, shape conditions, build connections, and strengthen the field infrastructure needed to deliver on the promise of a nation where all can thrive. We are relentlessly focused on delivering results at a scale that matches the challenges — and the potential — of this country. To Achieve This Vision, We Concentrate Our Efforts In Five Key Arenas That Shape How The Nation Governs And Delivers For Its People The legal and regulatory systems that shape our democracy and economy, including amending and advancing the U.S. Constitution Federal agencies, which are the engines of public policy and implementation that must deliver results State and local governments that deliver the benefits of citizenship to our everyday lives in the cities and states where we live We the People — whose collective consciousness and action must be activated toward everyone’s thriving The institutional leaders, decision makers, and capital allocators who hold disproportionate influence over how the nation governs Across these arenas, we are working to awaken a shared consciousness that embraces the humanity of every person; to shape government to deliver results for all; and to align the full force of our public, private, and civic resources to realize this future. To learn more about what we do and our mission visit our website . Joining a high-impact team with a $50 million annual budget and a staff of 70+ professionals , you will act as a chief architect of the donor experience, operating with the autonomy to drive strategy and align internal functions within a high-impact team. Reporting to the Chief Development Officer, you will be instrumental in orchestrating the strategy development and execution for our historic $1 billion, 10-year capital campaign. This role requires a sophisticated leader who moves beyond functional management to provide high-level stakeholder coordination and the oversight of a donor advisory council. You will lead a dynamic portfolio of initiatives designed to deepen relationships with institutional, corporate, and ultra-high-net-worth (UHNW) partners, ensuring all internal pieces move in lockstep with our collective campaign goals. This role will be essential in managing complex priorities and transforming the practice of philanthropy into a powerful force for whole-system change. Responsibilities Include But Are Not Limited To Strategic Philanthropy and Campaign Management Drive the enterprise-wide engagement strategy to deepen multi-dimensional relationships with institutional, UHNW, and corporate partners. Orchestrate the strategic roadmap for the $1B, 10-year capital campaign, ensuring organizational readiness for the early 2027 launch. Co-lead the architecture of campaign infrastructure, including the design of donor recognition levels, gift societies, and long-term stewardship frameworks. Govern a strategic portfolio of principal relationships, ensuring long-term alignment with the organization’s growth and campaign objectives. Design high-level campaign readiness initiatives, including regional salons and leadership briefings, to cultivate and secure foundational lead investors. Conceptualize and oversee signature leadership gatherings that enhance organizational visibility and serve as catalysts for campaign momentum. Steward complex corporate and philanthropic partnerships, ensuring a rigorous match between investor goals and PolicyLink’s systemic change mission. Direct large-scale operational ecosyste

title: Industrial Sensors CEO or Executive Chairman
description: Opportunity Description Our private equity client is looking for a professional in the Industrial IoT Sensor Manufacturing business with deep CEO/C- Level operational experience and expertise. Focus on manufacturers of components for measuring flow, position, force gas and others. This opportunity is for a new platform opportunity, and not for an existing portfolio company of the PE client. If you are looking for an immediate paid operating position, this program does not offer that and will not be a fit with your search. Prefer candidates who have an industry buy & build thesis starting with a foundational platform buy, and possibly target acquisitions identified. The role is to help with due diligence and take a senior role (most likely as CEO or Chairman) in a potential new acquisition to be found in collaboration with the executive. Please note: Our PE clients are control group buyers of businesses, they value companies based on a multiple of TTM EBITDA. They do not invest in startups nor early-stage companies. Qualifications Must have at least 10 years of CEO and/or C-Level operational experience with full P&L responsibility, specifically in Industrial IoT with sensor technology. Must have 10 years’ history of full P&L responsibility of at least $100MM Must have high EQ and leadership skills Must be willing to stay on post-acquisition in a leadership role Prefer prior Private Equity Portfolio Company experience at the C-Level Our client is seeking an executive who thinks like a buyer, and who has their own strong belief in a buy and build strategy and rationale for a particular niche in this industry. Please apply to only to this posting. Do not contact the Auctus team directly.

title: Senior Visual Brand Designer
description: Senior Visual Designer -  Klimt & Design Klimt & Design is a branding and product design agency specializing in building brands for technology companies from pre-seed and Series A to larger, more mature enterprises. Our clients range from high-growth startups to established global enterprises across sectors such as AI, enterprise SaaS, fintech and healthcare, and our work spans brand creation, rebranding, landing pages, marketing design, and product design. We are seeking a full-time, fully remote, senior-level visual brand designer to join our team. The Role We are seeking a full-time, senior-level visual brand designer to join our team. This is a client-facing delivery lead position where you will own multiple accounts end-to-end. You will execute the heavy-lifting strategic brand explorations while managing, mentoring, and delegating production tasks to our highly capable mid-level design team. Responsibilities Take ownership of client visual brand design projects, acting as the senior creative that clients experience as the lead. Lead the exploratory zone of brand systems and refreshes, executing competitive research, mood boards, positioning, and defining look, feel, and tone. Present explorations and application mockups to clients on review calls, guiding the team through incorporating feedback successfully. Delegate the extension of the design system (e.g., sizing ads, building out website pages, creating presentation decks) to mid-level designers. Lead daily team standups and act as the first point of contact for mid-level designers needing direction or unblocking. Own final quality assurance, catching issues at your level and ensuring strict process discipline for file hygiene and versioning. Anticipate and flag project risks (timeline, scope, missing inputs) and come to the table with proposed solutions. Oversee and occasionally execute sales enablement collateral (such as pitch decks and slide templates), defining the visual strategy before delegating the broader build-out to the team. Requirements Proven experience working autonomously in a senior design role, comfortable defining a brand system from very little initial direction. A standout portfolio demonstrating core competency in visual identity, brand design, and excellent creative thinking. High comfort level running client review calls and articulating and defending design decisions. Strong ability to context-switch across multiple accounts – ranging from earlier-stage tech companies to mature clients – at different stages of delivery. Proficiency in Figma and all industry-standard design tools. A strong commitment to mentorship; making the people around you better must be viewed as a core part of the job, not a bonus. Excellent fluency in English (verbal, comprehension, and communication). A mindset of "initiative over permission," where you proactively ask questions to fill gaps and recommend solutions rather than waiting to be told what to do. To apply, please submit a link to your portfolio or a few work samples highlighting your experience as a senior creative lead. We look forward to hearing from you!
score_reason: Senior remote role with strong brand creative fit, but agency delivery model and lack of integrated campaign/AI scope creates meaningful gap versus Travis's national CD background at major brands.

title: Creative Director, Lifecycle & Organic Growth
description: Why We Exist More than 80% of Americans own a car and see it as a fundamental necessity, yet the experience of owning one is stuck in the 90s. Every part of the user journey (shopping for a car, insurance, repairs, etc) is complicated, fragmented, and time-consuming, and the costs are staggering. In 2025, the cost of owning a car was more than 20% of the average household income, after tax. At Jerry.ai, we are building the first app to manage it all and make car ownership accessible to all. Why We Need You Since we launched our app in 2019, we’ve reached 5M customers, raised over $240M in funding, scaled our annual revenue more than 70X, and became profitable in early 2024 – just 7 years into our operating history, in spite of challenging macro conditions and in a hyper-competitive $2T U.S. market. We did this with an exceptionally talented team of analytical thinkers who are brilliant at experimentation and growth optimization. But what we don’t have is someone who obsesses over every single word we put in front of our customers and how we make them feel. Car ownership is a stressful and emotionally charged topic for most people, and right now, our messaging is very transactional (e.g. click here, shop with us). We need you to change that. Your job will be to think about what our customers actually need (or want) to hear from us, and how to fundamentally change how we speak to and care for them. Whether you’re crafting email sequences, push notifications, navigating a subreddit thread, or shaping how we talk about a new feature launch, you will be the voice of Jerry. And you will help us earn our brand and cultivate an enduring community. We spent the last 9 years building a product that people like, now we need to give them a reason to trust us and a reason to stay. Why You Will (or Won’t) Like Working Here Blank canvas: You won’t inherit a tone of voice or brand playbook. You will build it in partnership with our CEO, including defining our personality and voice across all organic surfaces. Hands-on: This is a senior level role in scope and impact, but you will be an IC with no direct reports on day one. Car ownership is not “sexy”: Insurance, maintenance, and safety are only topics people think about when they have a bill to pay, a flat tire to change, or when they get into an accident. Anyone can market something inherently exciting, but can you build enthusiasm and trust around a “dry” topic? What You’ll Own We need someone who knows how to talk about a brand in ways that make people feel something and take notice. Phase 1: Your sole focus will be overhauling our existing customer communications, including our CRM, email, SMS, and in-app copy. You will take our messaging from functional and direct to value-additive and relationship-driven. You will work closely with our Product, Design, Growth, and Customer Insights team to understand how to build trust, engagement, and as a result, drive long-tail conversion. Phase 2: As our tone of voice begins to take shape and we see success with lifecycle campaigns, your scope may expand as you take on driving customer growth and building brand affinity through producing other creative organic content (e.g. social media, etc). Who You Are A copywriting virtuoso: You know that every word matters. You can translate complex, dry, and even stressful topics (car insurance, loans, repairs) into copy that is engaging, human, and accessible. A T-Shaped marketer: Your deep expertise is in copywriting, audience development and product positioning. Horizontally, you have a great sense of the social media landscape and community dynamics (like Reddit). A first principles thinker: You avoid making assumptions. You know how to take qualitative data and user pain points and turn them into resonant, empathetic marketing copy. Comfortable with discomfort: You’ve weathered fast-growing startups before and can confidently push back on highly analytical, data-first teams to champion the unmeasurable va
score_reason: Senior creative scope on lifecycle/organic growth with strong emotional/messaging mandate, but DTC/growth-focused rather than integrated campaigns; no explicit remote confirmation or location details provided.

title: Creative Director, Lifecycle & Organic Growth
description: This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Creative Director, Lifecycle & Organic Growth in United States. This is a high-impact creative leadership role focused on redefining how a fast-scaling consumer platform communicates with its users across lifecycle and organic channels. You will be responsible for transforming functional, transactional messaging into emotionally resonant, trust-building storytelling that improves engagement and long-term retention. Acting as the voice of the brand, you will shape how millions of customers experience communications across email, SMS, push notifications, and in-app messaging. The role sits at the intersection of copywriting excellence, product marketing, and growth strategy, requiring both creative intuition and analytical awareness. You will partner closely with cross-functional teams including product, design, growth, and customer insights to build a cohesive tone of voice from the ground up. This is a hands-on, senior-level individual contributor role with significant influence on brand perception, customer trust, and lifecycle performance. Accountabilities Lead the transformation of lifecycle and CRM communications across email, SMS, push, and in-app messaging, shifting tone from transactional to human-centered and value-driven. Define and evolve the brand voice across organic and lifecycle channels, ensuring consistency, clarity, and emotional resonance. Collaborate with product, design, growth, and insights teams to translate user behavior and data into compelling, empathetic messaging strategies. Develop creative frameworks and messaging systems that improve engagement, trust, and long-term customer retention. Own copy development for customer journeys, feature launches, and ongoing communications across lifecycle stages. Expand scope into broader organic growth channels, including social media and community-driven platforms, as brand voice matures. Use qualitative insights, customer pain points, and behavioral data to shape narratives that strengthen brand affinity and customer relationships. Contribute to strategy discussions on how storytelling and copy can drive measurable business and growth outcomes. Requirements 7+ years of experience in creative marketing, copywriting, or lifecycle/organic growth roles within consumer-focused brands. Strong portfolio demonstrating excellence in copywriting, tone development, and customer-facing messaging across multiple channels. Deep expertise in CRM, lifecycle marketing, email, SMS, and organic content strategy. Ability to translate complex or emotionally sensitive topics into clear, engaging, and human-centered messaging. Strong understanding of audience development, community dynamics, and modern social platforms. Experience working in fast-paced, high-growth environments with cross-functional collaboration. First-principles thinker with strong qualitative reasoning and customer empathy. Comfortable influencing analytical or data-driven stakeholders while advocating for brand and creative excellence. Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field preferred. Benefits Competitive base salary ranging from $135,000 to $185,000, plus equity opportunities. Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance coverage. Paid time off, parental leave, and wellness benefits. 401(k) plan (U.S.) or RRSP (Canada) with employer matching. Flexible remote work environment across multiple U.S. locations. Employee equity grants as part of total compensation. Opportunity to shape a brand voice from the ground up in a high-growth, well-funded company. Access to a collaborative, innovation-driven culture focused on creativity, experimentation, and impact. How Jobgether Works We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. title: Head of Brand & Creative
description: I'm Karla Anderson, EVP of Marketing at Teamworks. We operate the leading sports technology platform serving professional, collegiate, Olympic, and military sports organizations worldwide. Our brand has a strong foundation and a clear point of view, and I'm now looking for the creative brand leader who will take it further. The Teamworks brand has clarity. There is real creative territory here that has not been fully explored yet. What I'm building toward is a brand and creative function that operates with premium quality, uses AI to scale efficiently, has built design systems to support efficient work across the company, connects the brand experience across each touchpoint both internally, externally, and within the product, and tells a richer story about who Teamworks is and where we're headed. This role is the person who makes that happen. The Role Own the Teamworks visual brand strategy across all three pillars: marketing brand, product brand, and corporate and employer brand, setting the direction and holding the standard across every surface Lead design systems governance as living infrastructure, including documentation, versioning, and source-of-truth ownership, building the foundation that enables AI to scale design quality across the company with confidence Direct creative output across campaigns, web, digital, social, events, and sales enablement, setting the quality bar and ensuring brand consistency across everything that faces the market Shape and build the creative and design function for the next phase of Teamworks, including directing the work of two junior designers and defining the team structure and talent roadmap going forward Partner directly with Marketing, Product Design, and occasionally executive leadership to ensure the brand shows up at the right quality across all touchpoints, from product interfaces to investor and employer brand Establish creative workflows, intake processes, and project management practices that allow the team to operate efficiently at scale—and raise a flag when brand decisions are being made without sufficient creative input What I'm Looking For What You Must Bring 5+ years of brand design, creative direction, or brand management experience, with meaningful time in B2B or vertical SaaS environments Demonstrated ability to personally design and produce work in Figma, not just art-direct and approve; this role requires someone who can open a file, produce real work, and lead by example when the team needs it End-to-end ownership of a brand system as governed infrastructure, including documentation, versioning, and cross-functional adoption at a company of comparable size and complexity Portfolio that demonstrates systems-level thinking alongside strong craft and taste, not just beautiful executions without the architecture behind them Ability to distill a creative vision into marketing campaigns and new, innovative ideas that push the brand forward Experience working cross-functionally with influence across each area of the business, operating as a true brand steward who sets and maintains the standard across teams Experience operating in a lean, resource-constrained environment where output expectations are high;
