title: Senior Creative Strategist
description: If you have not directly led performance creative that scaled a brand from low–mid 7 figures to 8 or 9 figures in revenue, this role is not a fit. If you have, and want the next big growth + scale challenge, read on. About AutoReel AutoReel is the leading AI-powered platform that transforms real estate listing photos into cinematic property videos in minutes. We serve thousands of users — from independent agents to major brokerages. Our platform enables real estate professionals (realtors, brokers, real estate photographers and media companies) to create professional property videos without the high cost and time associated with traditional video production. We’re a small, fast-moving team that ships constantly, with the leadership team from world-class companies such as Facebook, Instagram, and Snapchat. We’re scaling rapidly and need a creative leader who can build the creative engine that fuels our paid growth across Meta Ads, YouTube, and beyond. About the Role We’re looking for a Head of Performance Creative to own the creative strategy and production behind AutoReel’s paid acquisition. This is a hands-on leadership role where you’ll build the systems, build and manage a lean team (primarily offshore), and personally own the creative ads output that powers our growth. You’ll be responsible for producing 50-100+ video ads per week across channels, building scalable creative testing frameworks, and integrating AI tools into every stage of the workflow. This needs to be a thoughtful, scalable system that can support scaling up to 100-500 creatives weekly if needed. You understand how Meta and TikTok algorithms reward creative, and you know how to turn that knowledge into lower CPAs and higher ROAS. This isn’t a “big team, big budget” role. It’s a “scrappy, high-output, build-the-machine” role at a startup that’s growing fast and competing hard. What You’ll Do Own performance creative strategy: Set the creative direction and testing roadmap on Meta. You’ll decide what gets made, why, and how we measure success. Build and run the creative engine: Design the workflows, templates, feedback & learning systems that let us produce 50+ high-quality ad creatives per week without burning out – and support scaling up the number of creatives weekly as we grow. Lead with AI: Integrate AI tools into every stage of creative production — from ideation and scripting to editing and iteration. Maximize throughput while keeping quality high. Manage a small, high-performing team of creatives (as needed) Drive creative testing at scale: Build structured testing frameworks (hooks, formats, messaging angles, audiences) and turn data into actionable creative briefs. Partner with Growth: Work closely with our paid media buyer and creative production team to align creative strategy with campaign performance. Turn spend data into better ads. title: Creative Director, Lifecycle & Organic Growth
description: Why We Exist More than 80% of Americans own a car and see it as a fundamental necessity, yet the experience of owning one is stuck in the 90s. Every part of the user journey (shopping for a car, insurance, repairs, etc) is complicated, fragmented, and time-consuming, and the costs are staggering. In 2025, the cost of owning a car was more than 20% of the average household income, after tax. At Jerry.ai, we are building the first app to manage it all and make car ownership accessible to all. Why We Need You Since we launched our app in 2019, we’ve reached 5M customers, raised over $240M in funding, scaled our annual revenue more than 70X, and became profitable in early 2024 – just 7 years into our operating history, in spite of challenging macro conditions and in a hyper-competitive $2T U.S. market. We did this with an exceptionally talented team of analytical thinkers who are brilliant at experimentation and growth optimization. But what we don’t have is someone who obsesses over every single word we put in front of our customers and how we make them feel. Car ownership is a stressful and emotionally charged topic for most people, and right now, our messaging is very transactional (e.g. click here, shop with us). We need you to change that. Your job will be to think about what our customers actually need (or want) to hear from us, and how to fundamentally change how we speak to and care for them. Whether you’re crafting email sequences, push notifications, navigating a subreddit thread, or shaping how we talk about a new feature launch, you will be the voice of Jerry. And you will help us earn our brand and cultivate an enduring community. We spent the last 9 years building a product that people like, now we need to give them a reason to trust us and a reason to stay. Why You Will (or Won’t) Like Working Here Blank canvas: You won’t inherit a tone of voice or brand playbook. You will build it in partnership with our CEO, including defining our personality and voice across all organic surfaces. Hands-on: This is a senior level role in scope and impact, but you will be an IC with no direct reports on day one. Car ownership is not “sexy”: Insurance, maintenance, and safety are only topics people think about when they have a bill to pay, a flat tire to change, or when they get into an accident. Anyone can market something inherently exciting, but can you build enthusiasm and trust around a “dry” topic? What You’ll Own We need someone who knows how to talk about a brand in ways that make people feel something and take notice. Phase 1: Your sole focus will be overhauling our existing customer communications, including our CRM, email, SMS, and in-app copy. You will take our messaging from functional and direct to value-additive and relationship-driven. You will work closely with our Product, Design, Growth, and Customer Insights team to understand how to build trust, engagement, and as a result, drive long-tail conversion. Phase 2: As our tone of voice begins to take shape and we see success with lifecycle campaigns, your scope may expand as you take on driving customer growth and building brand affinity through producing other creative organic content (e.g. social media, etc). Who You Are A copywriting virtuoso: You know that every word matters. You can translate complex, dry, and even stressful topics (car insurance, loans, repairs) into copy that is engaging, human, and accessible. A T-Shaped marketer: Your deep expertise is in copywriting, audience development and product positioning. Horizontally, you have a great sense of the social media landscape and community dynamics (like Reddit). A first principles thinker: You avoid making assumptions. You know how to take qualitative data and user pain points and turn them into resonant, empathetic marketing copy. Comfortable with discomfort: You’ve weathered fast-growing startups before and can confidently push back on highly analytical, data-first teams to champion the unmeasurable va

title: Fractional CMO
description: FRACTIONAL CMO Plum Good Marketing | adj: very good. worth having.™ Plum Good Marketing is hiring a Fractional CMO. The role goes to someone who has spent five to eight years getting good at owning client relationships and is ready to step into the marketing seat at our clients’ leadership tables. You'll own your clients' marketing strategy. You'll attend their leadership meetings. You'll connect every marketing tactic to a business outcome they actually care about. You’re not someone who takes orders, you take ownership . That's the job. And it’s really fun and rewarding. This is also a development role. You're not expected to walk in carrying a full client load on day one. The first months are training, shadowing, and onboarding into Plum's voice, methodology, and the way we operate inside our clients' businesses. ABOUT PLUM We're a fractional CMO firm based in Metro Detroit. Our clients are growth-ready manufacturers, EOS-aligned leadership teams, and second-generation business owners. They have execution happening everywhere and strategic marketing ownership nowhere. They need us to fill that very important seat. We're technically an agency, but we don’t act like one. We operate inside our clients' businesses as the marketing department they always wished they had. Our seven core values are very well thought out and actionable in everything we do. We came up with them as a team and they're the filters we use for hiring, operating, and choosing clients: We are Reliable : We do what we say. Clients never have to chase us. We are Strategic : Strategy is our love language. Every tactic ties to a business outcome, or we don't do it. We are Experts : We've held our own in big rooms with smart people, and we belong there. When we know something, we say it with conviction. When we don't, we ask without being ashamed. We have EQ : Behind every frustrated client is a business owner who's poured everything into what they've built. We meet them there first. We are Thoughtful : We push back when something isn't in a client's best interest, or when "going above and beyond" has quietly turned into going at our own expense. Thoughtful means intentional, not slow. We are Worth It : We don't deliver work that isn't worth someone's time, attention, or money. Full presence is part of the value, and we know our value. We are Integrative : We don't operate in silos. Plum isn't a vendor you hand a task to. We're the connective tissue between vision and results. We're also operationally obsessed. We’re proud to have clear workflows and actual SOPs. And, we love Asana. We have written etiquette agreements for how we work together. We are utterly allergic to manufactured emergencies and chaos. We work remotely across different schedules. We assume positive intent. We don't manufacture fire drills. We hold each other accountable firmly and kindly. Full presence for our clients starts with full presence for ourselves. We run on EOS internally. Level 10s, rocks, scorecards, V/TO. If those words mean nothing to you yet, that’s ok, you'll learn them. If they're already part of your vocabulary, we love that too! WHAT YOU'LL DO You own your clients' marketing leadership. That means: Creating quarterly strategies connected to their business goals. Participating in leadership meetings (L10s for our EOS clients) as the marketing expert. Coordinating with the teams executing the marketing work, whether that’s the amazing delivery team at Plum, your clients’ internal marketing employees, or the agencies they’ve hired. Owning the rocks (goals), the roadmap, the reporting, and the relationship Your clients will fall into three patterns: manufacturers with direct-to-consumer channels, B2B and niche manufacturing companies running on EOS, and second-generation owners modernizing legacy brands. ABOUT YOU You probably look something like this: Five to eight years in account management, brand leadership, agency operations, or in-house marketing director roles A histo

title: Creative Director Benevity · Canada (Remote) Actively recruiting
description: Meet Benevity Benevity is the way the world does good, providing companies (and their employees) with technology to take social action on the issues they care about. Through giving, volunteering, grantmaking, employee resource groups and micro-actions, we help most of the Fortune 100 brands build better cultures and use their power for good. We’re also one of the first B Corporations in Canada, meaning we’re as committed to purpose as we are to profits. We have people working all over the world, including Canada, Spain, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, the United States and more! High-Level Overview Are you a visionary creative ready to help shape the visual identity of a global brand? As our Creative Director, you will be the guardian of the Benevity brand, responsible for defining and evolving our brand’s visual and digital language to inspire action and drive social impact. You are equally at home concepting a campaign as you are building a project plan — a rare blend of creative and operational leader who thrives on both the big idea and the details that bring it to life. You will work at the intersection of strategy and execution, leading high-stakes creative projects and mentoring designers to elevate our profile as a category leader and the world's enterprise impact platform. This role is ideal for a leader who "grew up digital” and knows how to run a tight creative operation. Managing the processes, contributing to the work and mentoring designers to push their craft, leading high-stakes creative projects, and evolving our brand’s visual language to inspire action and drive global social impact. If you’re ready to take your creative career to the next level, we would love to hear from you! What You'll Do Creative Strategy: Develop and implement a comprehensive brand design strategy aligned with Benevity's business objectives to improve brand equity and visual consistency across all touchpoints. Visual Identity Leadership: Lead the evolution of Benevity’s visual identity system, across all media, ensuring it remains modern, accessible, and deeply resonant with our global audience. Creative Execution: Lead day to day execution and output of creative team and brainstorming for major marketing programs, product launches, and events. Develop a creative base for “big Ideas” that convert product and social impact data into emotional, thumb-stopping creative concepts. Direct, design and produce high-quality creative assets for major campaigns, product launches, and brand initiatives, ensuring excellence in typography, color, engagement (digital) and layout. Operational Leadership & Workload Management: Own the creative team's intake process, project prioritization, and capacity planning to ensure the right work gets done at the right time and that our teams can work at the speed of impact. Build and maintain systems that give the team clarity on priorities, deadlines, and bandwidth — balancing urgent requests against longer-horizon brand-building work without sacrificing quality. Collaboration across the business: Partner with Product, Marketing, Sales and People teams to ensure appropriate input and creative briefs that lend themselves to a unified brand experience across the entire customer and employee journey. Work with and influence stakeholders at all levels, including executives. Web oversight: Partner with the Digital Marketing team to deliver a high-performing, on-brand web experience, establishing and enforcing shared standards that drive improvements in performance, accessibility, and creative consistency. Mentorship & Influence: Provide creative direction and mentorship to the design team, supporting their design time while fostering a culture of innovation, excellence, experimentation and continuous learning. Team and Agency Management: In addition to managing internal team resources, oversee budget and external creative agencies and vendors to ensure timely project execution that meets Benevity’s high standards f
score_reason: Senior CD, remote, Canada-based, but lacking explicit USD/CAD compensation and unclear if scope matches Travis's integrated campaign + AI expertise at enterprise scale.

title: Creative Lead, Lifecycle & Organic Growth
description: This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Creative Lead, Lifecycle & Organic Growth in Canada. This is an exciting opportunity for a creative and strategic marketer who thrives at the intersection of storytelling, customer engagement, and organic growth. In this role, you will shape the voice and emotional connection of a fast-growing consumer platform used by millions of customers. You’ll lead the evolution of lifecycle communications across email, SMS, in-app messaging, and emerging organic channels, transforming transactional interactions into meaningful brand experiences. Working closely with product, growth, design, and customer insights teams, you’ll craft messaging that builds trust, drives retention, and strengthens long-term customer loyalty. This position offers a highly autonomous and collaborative environment where creativity, experimentation, and first-principles thinking are valued. It’s an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys building from the ground up and making a visible impact on brand perception and customer growth. Accountabilities Lead the development and evolution of brand voice, tone, and messaging across lifecycle and organic growth channels. Create and optimize customer communications including email campaigns, SMS messaging, push notifications, in-app copy, onboarding flows, and retention programs. Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Product, Design, Growth, and Customer Insights to align messaging with user needs and business goals. Build customer trust and engagement through empathetic, human-centered storytelling and value-driven content. Analyze customer feedback, behavioral insights, and qualitative research to improve messaging effectiveness and customer experience. Develop and execute creative organic growth initiatives across channels such as social media, online communities, and content-driven engagement platforms. Contribute to customer retention, brand affinity, and long-term conversion through thoughtful and emotionally resonant communication strategies. Continuously experiment with messaging approaches, audience segmentation, and creative formats to optimize lifecycle performance. Requirements 5+ years of experience in creative marketing, lifecycle marketing, copywriting, or organic growth roles within consumer-focused environments. Strong expertise in writing compelling copy for email, SMS, in-app experiences, social media, and customer engagement campaigns. Proven ability to translate complex or technical topics into clear, engaging, and relatable messaging. Experience building or evolving brand voice and customer communication strategies in fast-paced or high-growth organizations. Deep understanding of audience engagement, community dynamics, and organic growth channels. Strong collaboration and communication skills with the ability to work effectively across product, marketing, and design teams. Analytical mindset with the ability to leverage qualitative insights and customer behavior data to inform creative decisions. Comfortable operating independently in a hands-on individual contributor role with significant ownership and autonomy. Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience preferred. Portfolio demonstrating creative writing, lifecycle campaigns, and brand storytelling experience required. Benefits Competitive base salary ranging from CA$125,000 to CA$175,000 annually depending on experience, skills, and location. Equity opportunities as part of the total compensation package. Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance coverage. RRSP plan with employer matching contributions. Paid time off and paid parental leave programs. Wellness benefits and flexible remote work environment. Access to professional growth opportunities within a high-growth technology company. Collaborative and innovation-driven culture with significant ownership and impact. How Jobgether Works We use an AI-powered matc

You’re relentlessly expanding your skills, your vision, and your impact. INCLUSION, BELONGING & IMPACT Our workplace is for ALL. We foster a culture where every background and perspective is valued so we can create meaningful memories that resonate with everyone.
